_publications._publicationTitle: An inflammatory gene signature distinguishes neurofibroma Schwann cells and macrophages from cells in the normal peripheral nervous system.

_contextSnippet: This treatment reduced levels of IFN-γ, IL1B, and CSF1 to, or close to, levels present in wild-type nerve ( Fig. 8a ). MEK inhibition shrinks 75% of neurofibromas, while PEGylated IFN-α2b does not shrink tumors significantly
details: Treatment at 10,000 IU weekly for 8 weeks reduced levels of IFN-γ, IL1B, and CSF1 to or close to levels present in wild-type nerve, but did not significantly shrink tumors
